questions.question: Did the applicant include a Project Narrative?

questions.question: Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)

Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?

questions.question: Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in the assessment period on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target?
Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT?
Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MSAW, HP, RPH) from the SAAT?
Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each population type listed in the SAAT?

questions.suggestedResources: Form 5B: Service Sites[? | SAATE | Form 5B: Service Sites [" | Project Narrative [?

questions.question: New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? * If funding is only requested for MSAW, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.

New or competing supplement applicant requesting RPH funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?

New or competing supplement applicant requesting HP and/or RPH funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
